text: Writing clean code is an important skill for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand. One key practice is using clear naming conventions. Instead of naming a variable "x", call it "userAge" or "totalPrice". This helps anyone reading your code know what each part does instantly. It is also a good idea to break your code into small, simple functions that each do one thing. This makes the code easier to test and fix later. All these habits improve maintainability, meaning you or other developers can update the code without confusion. Clean code saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
